hazeSeasCodes.title: Haze Seas Codes
hazeSeasCodes.intro: Redeem Haze Seas codes from the in-game menu through the Treasure Chest or Gift code window for free cash, race spins, gems, EXP boosts, and stat resets. New codes usually tie to updates, trailers, community milestones, and group rewards.

items.name: GROUPONLY
items.description: Active — join the Haze Studios Roblox group

unlockMilestones: Launch Haze Seas from the official Roblox page
Select the Treasure Chest or Gift code icon
Paste the code exactly as written
Press Redeem and claim the reward

hazeSeasOfficialLinks.title: Haze Seas Official Links
hazeSeasOfficialLinks.intro: Haze Seas uses multiple official channels: Trello for structured game data, Discord for live announcements and fruit stock, Roblox for the game and group, YouTube for trailers and showcases, and X for quick update posts.

cards.name: Haze Seas Official Trello
cards.description: The main structured reference for Game Info, codes, controls, Chest System, Mastery System, Bounty System, Sea Events, Devil Fruits, Swords, Fighting Styles, Races, NPCs, Bosses, and locations.

cards.name: Haze Studios Discord
cards.description: The live community hub for game announcements, hotfixes, code drops, fruit stock tracking, questions, and bug reports.

cards.name: Haze Seas Roblox Game Page
cards.description: The official Roblox experience page for HAZE PIECE and the Haze Seas rework, including device support, game-pass notes, and fruit spawn information.

cards.name: Haze Studios Roblox Group
cards.description: The official Haze Studios community page on Roblox. Group membership also unlocks group-only rewards such as the GROUPONLY code.

cards.name: Haze Studios YouTube
cards.description: The official video channel for Haze Seas trailers, release videos, fruit awakening cutscenes, and gameplay showcases.

cards.name: Haze Seas X
cards.description: The official X account for quick Haze Seas announcements, trailer posts, showcase clips, and code-related update posts.

hazeSeasBeginnerGuide.title: Haze Seas Beginner Guide
hazeSeasBeginnerGuide.intro: Haze Seas is the Haze Studios Roblox action RPG rework built around islands, enemies, bosses, fruits, swords, fighting styles, races, mastery, bounty, and sea events. Use this starter flow to progress smoothly from your first island to late-game routes.

steps.title: Launch the Official Roblox Experience
steps.description: Start from the official HAZE PIECE Roblox page, which is tied to the Haze Seas rework by Haze Studios. Favorite or follow the experience before joining a server.

steps.title: Pick Your Device Setup
steps.description: The game page lists Xbox and mobile support, so you can start on PC, mobile, or console. Use the device you control most comfortably and check the Trello controls card before fighting bosses.

steps.title: Read Game Info Before Grinding
steps.description: The Trello Game Info column covers controls, codes, Chest System, Mastery System, and Bounty System. Check Game Info first, then branch into Devil Fruits, Swords, Fighting Styles, Races, NPCs, and Bosses.

steps.title: Claim Codes Early
steps.description: Codes reward cash, race spins, gems, EXP boosts, and stat resets, which matter most before a long grinding session. Open Menu, select the Treasure Chest or Gift code icon, paste a code, and redeem before leveling.

steps.title: Choose a Simple First Build
steps.description: Keep enough Defense to survive while choosing whether your main damage path will come from Fruit, Sword, Fighting Style, or Combat. Pick one main damage route and use Trello to compare related skills.

steps.title: Track Devil Fruits
steps.description: Fruits spawn every 60 minutes and despawn after 20 minutes. VIP servers can hold fruit spawns with a 5-fruit limit. Use the Discord fruit-stock channel and Trello fruit cards before swapping fruits.

steps.title: Use Discord for Live Game Status
steps.description: The Discord server separates announcements, codes, fruit stock, questions, and bug reports into channels. Check game-announcement for hotfixes, codes for rewards, and fruit-stock for tracking.

steps.title: Watch Official Showcases
steps.description: Haze Studios posts trailers, release videos, fruit awakening scenes, and showcase clips on YouTube. Use official videos to preview combat style, fruit animations, and update direction before committing to a build.

quickTips: Treat the official Trello and Discord as part of your core toolkit.
Pick one main damage route instead of spreading every stat evenly.
Check fruit stock on Discord before spending cash, gems, or rerolls.
Keep enough Defense to survive while your damage route comes online.

hazeSeasDevilFruits.title: Haze Seas Devil Fruits
hazeSeasDevilFruits.intro: Devil Fruits are one of the main build paths in Haze Seas. Fruit choices shape grinding speed, PvP pressure, mobility, and boss farming. Use this hard data to plan spawns, stock, and awakenings.

solutions.name: Fruit Spawn Timer
solutions.description: A fruit spawns on the map every 60 minutes. Check the map or stock channel around the hourly timer instead of searching randomly.

solutions.name: Fruit Despawn Timer
solutions.description: A spawned fruit despawns after 20 minutes. Move quickly after a fruit appears, especially on public servers where other players are also hunting.

solutions.name: VIP Server Fruit Limit
solutions.description: Fruits can spawn in VIP servers with a listed limit of 5. Use VIP servers for cleaner fruit hunting routes and fewer interruptions.

solutions.name: Fruit Stock Tracking
solutions.description: The official Discord includes a fruit-stock channel for live Devil Fruit stock tracking. Check stock before spending cash, gems, or rerolls.

solutions.name: Fruit Data Cards
solutions.description: The official Trello separates Devil Fruits into their own reference area alongside Swords, Fighting Styles, Races, NPCs, and Bosses. Open the fruit card before replacing your current fruit.

solutions.name: Known Fruit Roster
solutions.description: Clear, Chop, Spin, Spike, Bomb, Barrier, Paw, Smoke, Sand, String, Mammoth, Buddha, Snow, Tremor, Gas, Gravity, Flame, Shadow, Light, Operation, Ice, Electricity, Magma, Love, Gum, Darkness, Magnet, Phoenix, Soul, Leopard, Venom, Dragon, and Dough.

solutions.name: Awakenings
solutions.description: Haze Studios has published a Haze Seas fruit awakening cutscene video, making awakenings a front-page topic for fruit players.

solutions.name: Grinding Use
solutions.description: For leveling, players want fruit moves that clear groups, travel between islands, or damage bosses safely. Mark fruits by farming role after testing moves and cooldowns.

solutions.name: PvP Use
solutions.description: For PvP, fruit value depends on how well the move kit creates openings, extends combos, avoids punishment, or forces opponents to block. Separate PvP notes from grinding notes.

managementTips: Check the Discord fruit-stock channel before hunting on the map.
Use VIP servers for cleaner fruit routes and fewer interruptions.
Compare moves, mastery requirements, and awakenings on Trello before swapping.
Separate grinding fruits from PvP fruits when planning your build.

hazeSeasTierList.title: Haze Seas Tier List
hazeSeasTierList.intro: This tier list organizes the most searched build choices into separate groups so you can compare each system without mixing fruits, weapons, races, and styles. Use it as a practical reference when deciding what to keep, reroll, farm, or upgrade next.

achievements.title: Dough
achievements.description: S-tier mythical — PvP combos, boss damage, and late-game builds.

achievements.title: Dragon
achievements.description: S-tier mythical Zoan — bossing, transformation builds, and high-end PvP.

achievements.title: Phoenix
achievements.description: S-tier legendary — survival, sustain, and flexible PvE.

achievements.title: Leopard
achievements.description: A-tier legendary Zoan — aggressive melee pressure and PvP.

achievements.title: Venom
achievements.description: A-tier legendary — damage over time, bosses, and area control.

achievements.title: Magma
achievements.description: A-tier rare Logia — grinding, boss damage, and area attacks.

achievements.title: Light
achievements.description: A-tier rare Logia — mobility, fast progression, and ranged farming.

achievements.title: Yoru / Dark Blade
achievements.description: S-tier mythical sword — premium sword builds and PvP.

achievements.title: Enma
achievements.description: S-tier legendary sword — late-game sword damage from the Enma Boss route.

achievements.title: 3 Sword Style
achievements.description: S-tier legendary sword — combo-heavy sword builds from the 3SS Boss route.

achievements.title: Sea Beast Hammer V2
achievements.description: S-tier legendary weapon — sea-event farming from the White Sea Beast route.

achievements.title: Golden Staff
achievements.description: A-tier legendary sword — electricity-linked builds from the Thunder God route.

achievements.title: Shusui
achievements.description: A-tier legendary sword — Thriller Boat progression from the Ryummy route.

achievements.title: D. Clan
achievements.description: S-tier mythical — endgame PvP, mobility, and survivability with multiple premium bonuses.

achievements.title: Dragonoid
achievements.description: S-tier legendary — Observation Haki builds and durable combat.

achievements.title: Lunarian
achievements.description: S-tier legendary — mobility and sustain with regeneration and SkyWalk.

achievements.title: Demon
achievements.description: A-tier rare — fast aggression and PvP movement with a major speed bonus.

achievements.title: Celestial Dragon
achievements.description: A-tier rare — quest money and balanced progression.

achievements.title: Dragonic / Dragon Claw
achievements.description: S-tier advanced style — late-game melee pressure.

achievements.title: Cyborg
achievements.description: S-tier advanced style — burst damage and ability-based melee.

achievements.title: Fish-Man Karate
achievements.description: A-tier style — balanced PvE and PvP melee pressure.

achievements.title: Electro
achievements.description: A-tier style — speed-focused melee and stun pressure.

hazeSeasRaces.title: Haze Seas Races
hazeSeasRaces.intro: Races affect movement, health, stamina, regeneration, Haki access, and long-term build direction. Use early race spins carefully when deciding whether to keep a rare or legendary race for grinding, PvP, or sea progression.

regions.name: D. Clan
regions.description: X-shaped scar — health regen, walk speed, SkyWalk, Observation Haki, stamina, health, and Conquerors Spirit. Best for endgame PvP and mobility.

regions.name: Dragonoid
regions.description: Dragon wings and tail — health regen, Observation Haki, health, and jump power. Best for durable builds and late-game progression.

regions.name: Lunarian
regions.description: Black wings and fire orb — SkyWalk, health regen, jump power, and stamina. Best for mobility, sustain, and vertical movement.

regions.name: Demon
regions.description: Demon horns and black tail — walk speed, health, and stamina. Best for aggressive PvP movement and fast questing.

regions.name: Celestial Dragon
regions.description: Glass helmet — quest money, health, and stamina. Best for money farming and balanced progression.

regions.name: Mink
regions.description: Rabbit ears — walk speed, jump power, and stamina. Best for early movement and mobility-focused grinding.

regions.name: Fishman
regions.description: Shark fin — health and swim speed. Best for sea movement and island-to-island travel.

regions.name: Cyborg
regions.description: Science tank — health and health regeneration. Best for safer early combat and durability.

regions.name: Skypeian
regions.description: Angel wings — stamina and jump power. Best for jumping, stamina, and early movement.

regions.name: Human
regions.description: Default starter race with no major build-defining bonus. Reroll when spins are available.

regions.name: 3 Eyed Tribe
regions.description: Third eye — minor stamina bonus. Reroll when spins are available.

regions.name: Elf
regions.description: Elf ears — minor health regeneration bonus. Reroll when spins are available.

hazeSeasWeapons.title: Haze Seas Weapons and Fighting Styles
hazeSeasWeapons.intro: Weapons and fighting styles define how a build plays when fruit abilities are on cooldown or when you want a sword-focused route. Use this comparison to decide what to buy early, farm from bosses, and target for late-game PvP or sea events.

settings.name: Yoru / Dark Blade
settings.description: Shop, gifting, or trading route — premium PvP sword builds.

settings.name: Enma
settings.description: Defeat Enma Boss — late-game sword damage.

settings.name: Sea Beast Hammer V2
settings.description: Defeat White Sea Beast — sea-event weapon builds.

settings.name: Golden Staff
settings.description: Defeat Thunder God on Sky Islands — electricity-linked sword builds.

settings.name: Shusui
settings.description: Defeat Ryummy at Thriller Boat — late-game sword progression.

settings.name: Raiu
settings.description: Shiryu Scroll route at Skull Island — fast sword pressure.

settings.name: Bisento
settings.description: Purchase from Bella NPC for 500,000 Money — reliable purchased sword.

settings.name: Sea Beast Hammer
settings.description: Defeat Sea Beast at Rocky Pillars — heavy damage and sea-event farming.

settings.name: Mace
settings.description: Defeat Mace Boss on Winter Island — guaranteed heavy melee route.

settings.name: Black Leg
settings.description: Trainer route at Sea Restaurant — practical first melee upgrade.

settings.name: Electro
settings.description: Trainer route at Udon Prison — fast melee and stun pressure.

settings.name: Fish-Man Karate
settings.description: Trainer route on Fishman progression — balanced PvE and PvP melee.

settings.name: Cyborg
settings.description: Advanced trainer route — burst-focused ability-based melee.

settings.name: Dragonic / Dragon Claw
settings.description: Advanced trainer route — late-game aggressive melee pressure.

hazeSeasBosses.title: Haze Seas Bosses Islands and Sea Events
hazeSeasBosses.intro: Haze Seas progression is built around island routes, boss farming, item drops, super bosses, and sea events. Use this map and drop guide to decide what to farm next as you push from the First Sea into late-game routes.

entries.title: Shark Park — Shark Boss (Lv 120)
entries.description: Farm the Shark Blade drop here for an early rare sword before moving deeper into First Sea progression.

entries.title: Sea Restaurant — Krieg Boss (Lv 300)
entries.description: Drops Gold Pauldrons. A useful early stop for accessory farming and Black Leg progression.

entries.title: Logue Town — Tashii (Lv 400)
entries.description: Drops Glasses. Continue farming here before moving into mid-game boss routes.

entries.title: Sky Islands — Thunder God (Lv 1100)
entries.description: Drops Golden Staff and Thunder Drums. Farm for the Golden Staff route and electricity-themed progression.

entries.title: Fishman Island — Neptune (Lv 1700)
entries.description: Drops Neptune Crown and Fork. Farm for Fishman-related equipment routes.

entries.title: Skull Island — Shiryu (Lv 1850)
entries.description: Drops Scroll for Raiu progression. A key stop for late First Sea weapon routes.

entries.title: Thriller Boat — Ryummy (Lv 2150)
entries.description: Drops Shusui and Blue Scarf. A major late First Sea farming target for sword players.

entries.title: Logue City — Dual Swordsman (Lv 500)
entries.description: Drops 2 Sword Style V2. Farm after planning around 2 Sword Style progression.

entries.title: Super Boss — Tremor Girl
entries.description: Drops Bisento V2. A direct upgrade route for Bisento-style sword combat.

entries.title: Late Boss — Enma Boss
entries.description: Drops Enma. Farm when ready to chase a stronger legendary sword.

entries.title: Late Boss — 3SS Boss
entries.description: Drops 3 Sword Style. A key target for multi-sword and combo-heavy builds.

entries.title: Rocky Pillars — Sea Beast (Lv 700)
entries.description: Drops Sea Beast Hammer, Sea Beast Core, Money, Gems, and Bounty. Use for heavy weapon and material farming.

entries.title: Sea Route — White Sea Beast
entries.description: Drops Sea Beast Hammer V2. Farm for the upgraded Sea Beast Hammer path.

entries.title: Udon Prison — Mammoth Route
entries.description: Second Sea progression with an Electro trainer route for advanced melee.

entries.title: Venom Island — Venom Boss
entries.description: Venom route progression. Farm for late-game ability and boss progression.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Haze Seas

questions.question: What is Haze Seas?
questions.answer: Haze Seas is the Haze Studios Roblox pirate action RPG rework of HAZE PIECE, built around islands, Devil Fruits, swords, fighting styles, races, bosses, bounty, and sea events.

questions.question: How do I redeem Haze Seas codes?
questions.answer: Launch Haze Seas, open the Menu button, select the Treasure Chest or Gift code icon, paste a code exactly as written, and press Redeem to claim the reward on the same account.

questions.question: How often do Devil Fruits spawn in Haze Seas?
questions.answer: In Haze Seas, a Devil Fruit spawns every 60 minutes and despawns after 20 minutes. VIP servers can hold fruit spawns with a 5-fruit limit, and live stock is tracked in the Discord fruit-stock channel.

questions.question: Where can I find official Haze Seas links?
questions.answer: The official Haze Seas channels are the Trello board for structured data, the Haze Studios Discord for live updates, the Roblox game and group pages, the Haze Studios YouTube for trailers, and the Haze Seas X account for quick posts.

questions.question: How do races and race spins work in Haze Seas?
questions.answer: Haze Seas races affect movement, health, stamina, regeneration, and Haki access. Use race spins to reroll, and keep rare, legendary, or mythical rolls that match your grinding, PvP, or sea progression goals.

questions.question: Is Haze Seas free to play on Roblox?
questions.answer: Yes. Haze Seas is a Roblox experience playable on PC, mobile, and console through the official HAZE PIECE Roblox game page.
